iran.headline: Strait of Hormuz shipping traffic remains near standstill for over a week, with only Iran-linked vessels transiting amid ongoing conflict.
iran.summary: Shipping through the Strait of Hormuz has dropped to near zero for non-Iranian vessels, with only one Iran-linked bulk carrier exiting the Persian Gulf on March 8 and three ships total on March 9, including a sanctioned Iranian VLCC bound for China[2][3]. Traffic halted following attacks on merchant vessels starting late February, leading to port congestion, refinery cutbacks in Iraq, Kuwait, and UAE, and Saudi Arabia boosting Red Sea exports[2][3]. Large numbers of tankers are anchored outside the strait, with widespread AIS signal interference complicating tracking[1][2]. No reports of official closure by Iran, but security threats and insurance issues have deterred commercial traffic[1].

gulf_swf.headline: Saudi PIF unveils revised 2026-2030 strategy, pivoting to industry, minerals, AI, and tourism amid low oil prices, scaling back mega-projects like The Line.
gulf_swf.summary: Saudi Arabia's Public Investment Fund (PIF) announced a new five-year strategy for 2026-2030 at a Riyadh forum, shifting focus from costly giga-projects to sectors including industry, minerals, artificial intelligence, tourism, logistics, mining, advanced manufacturing, and clean energy.[1][3][4] The revision addresses financial pressures from low oil prices, with NEOM emphasizing renewable energy and industrial development over tourism and real estate.[1][3] PIF is enhancing private sector partnerships, having signed agreements worth over SAR 15 billion previously and offering new investment opportunities valued at SAR 40 billion.[2] No reports confirm Iran strikes on SWF infrastructure, Strait of Hormuz disruptions, or wartime stress impacting Gulf SWFs as of 2026-03-11.

israel.headline: Israel conducts ongoing airstrikes on Iran and Hezbollah targets amid full-scale war started February 28, 2026, destroying hundreds of Iranian missile launchers and striking 60 Hezbollah sites in Lebanon on March 3.
israel.summary: The US-Israel coalition launched Operation Roaring Lion on February 28, 2026, striking Iranian military sites, air defenses, missile launchers, and leadership, including killing Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei, with over 1,600 IAF sorties and 4,000 munitions dropped by March 3.[1][2][3][4][5] Iran retaliated with missile barrages on Israel, including hits near Tel Aviv, while Hezbollah resumed attacks on northern Israel and IDF positions in Lebanon, prompting IDF strikes on 60 Hezbollah targets and ground positioning along the Lebanon border.[1][2][3] IDF reported destroying 300 Iranian launchers, achieving a 70% drop in missile fire, and intercepted over 100 drones.[1][2]

oracle_ellison.headline: No new developments reported on Oracle financial stress, Stargate Abilene cancellation, bondholder lawsuit, or Ellison WBD guarantee as of March 11, 2026.
oracle_ellison.summary: Oracle stock price stood at approximately 165.85 USD on March 11, 2026, up 10.94% daily but down 4.94% over the past four weeks, with market cap around $460-475B.[1][2] Recent trading data shows volatility, with prices ranging from 141-150 USD in early March and no mentions of capital raises, debt crises, or bond market impacts tied to energy issues.[3][4][5] Searches yielded no updates on Stargate Abilene expansion cancellation, Ohio Carpenters Pension lawsuit, or Larry Ellison's personal guarantee for a Warner Bros Discovery acquisition.

epstein_files.headline: DOJ released three previously withheld 2019 FBI 302 reports alleging Trump abused a minor, amid accusations of improper withholding.
epstein_files.summary: The Department of Justice released three FBI 302 interview summaries from 2019 on March 5, 2026, detailing uncorroborated allegations by a woman against Donald Trump, previously marked as duplicative and withheld from January releases.[1][2] Senator Sheldon Whitehouse demanded preservation of remaining records, including 37 unreleased pages of FBI notes, accusing DOJ of illegal withholding under the Epstein Files Transparency Act.[2] DOJ also published over 3 million additional pages, including videos and images, in compliance with the Act signed in November 2025, while stating claims against Trump are baseless.[1][3] Congressional Democrats, including Robert Garcia, criticized AG Pam Bondi for the delays.[1]

arkansas_datacenter.headline: AVAIO Digital announced $6B initial investment in AI-ready data center hub in Pulaski County, Arkansas, on January 12, 2026, with construction starting Q1 2026.
arkansas_datacenter.summary: AVAIO Digital revealed plans for a 760-acre data center campus named AVAIO Digital Leo near Little Rock, marking the largest economic investment in Arkansas history, potentially scaling to $21B total.[1][2] The project includes a contract with Entergy Arkansas for 150 MW initial power, scaling to 1 GW, supported by recent state legislation reducing energy project timelines and offering data center incentives, as praised by Governor Sarah Huckabee Sanders.[2][3] First phase construction begins in Q1 2026, with energization by June 2027, expected to create over 500 permanent jobs.[1][4] No new developments on Good Day Farm ruling, ballot initiative restrictions, or DATA Act of 2026 reported since.

convergence_note: 8 nodes active simultaneously — within the 7-day convergence window defined by the thermostat model.
